The '''Ground Armored Tank (GAT)''' was a light tank used in the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] by the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]]. It was rivaled by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] [[TX-130 Saber-class fighter tank/Legends|TX-130 ''Saber''-class fighter tank]].

==Description==
The vehicle's armament consisted of two medium [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] and two missile launchers. It was built by [[Baktoid Armor Workshop/Legends|Baktoid Armor Workshop]] and, like many Separatist vehicles, the GAT was [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] by an integrated [[Droid/Legends|droid]] brain. While it was capable of performing a variety of tasks, the GAT excelled as a reconnaissance and fast-attack craft, due to its light armor.

==History==
[[File:GAT.jpg|thumb|left|220px|The Ground Armored Tank]]
The GAT received heavy use early on in the Clone Wars, especially in the [[Dark Reaper crisis]], serving alongside the larger [[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|Armored Assault Tank]]. It had light armor, and its high speed made it excellent for hit and run attacks. It was often used in conjunction with the [[Single Trooper Aerial Platform/Legends|Single Trooper Aerial Platform]]. They were deployed during the [[Battle of Tirahnn (Clone Wars)|Battle of Tirahnn]].<ref name="GAW">''[[Galaxy at War]]''</ref>

Following the execution of the [[Separatist Council/Legends|Separatist Council]] by [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious's]] new [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], these units were de-activated.

==Appearances==
*[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (video game)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' video game]] {{1st}}
